projectInfo.title: Science on Wheels for Local to Global Impact
projectInfo.subtitle: Mobile Science Van Initiative across Chhattisgarh, Punjab and Himachal Pradesh

mission.targetAudience: Students from Classes 6-12 in rural, border, remote and tribal areas

states.name: Chhattisgarh
states.focus: Tribal and forested districts with ST populations exceeding 45–60%

states.name: Punjab
states.focus: International border districts and SC-dominated rural villages

states.name: Himachal Pradesh
states.focus: Hill villages with tribal populations and minimal hands-on science exposure

modules.name: Space Tech Education
modules.description: Hands-on space technology equipment, rocketry models and remote sensing demonstrations that connect students to India's space missions.

modules.name: Robotics Station
modules.description: A robotics station with real-world applications where students build, program and experiment with robots.

modules.name: Water Testing Station
modules.description: Field water-quality testing kits that let students analyse local water sources and link science to community health.

modules.name: Sustainability Models
modules.description: Interactive sustainability education models on energy, environment and climate, rooted in local challenges.

objectives: Increase student engagement and interest in robotics, space knowledge, environmental science and sustainability, specifically in rural and remote areas
Have students identify the role of science in addressing local challenges
Enhance students' scientific skills through hands-on activities

rows.details: Plan van visits to Rajnandgaon, Mungeli, Jashpur and other rural, tribal and remote districts. Finalize schools and develop materials with Block Education Officers (BEOs). Target 45–50 school engagements.
Take the van to permitted schools in rural / SC / ST districts. Assess impact.
Plan for schools in rural, tribal and remote areas around Mandi and Kullu. Aim for 45–50 schools with district administrators and BEOs.
Take the van to permitted rural / tribal area schools. Assess impact.
Plan visits to border, rural and remote districts such as Faridkot, Fazilka, Ferozepur. Target 45–50 school engagements with BEOs.
Take the van to permitted remote, border and rural schools. Assess impact.
Plan van visits to Kangra, Hamirpur, Bilaspur interiors. Visit 45–50 schools. Pre-coordinate with BEOs for seasonal accessibility.
Take the van to permitted schools.
Prepare for Pathankot, Hoshiarpur, Mansa. Coordinate with local BEOs to schedule science sessions in 45–50 schools.
Villages and border region outreach to SC/ST schools; science fairs and public exhibits.
Plan second phase in Kanker, Gariyaband and other districts. Plan 45–50 school visits with prior BEO coordination and approval.
Take the van to tribal-dominated, remote schools and run follow-up sessions in new districts.

legacy.title: Punjab 2025–26: Our Track Record
legacy.description: Between October 2025 and February 2026, Science on Wheels reached PM Shri schools across Punjab with three vans running in parallel.

idym.name: Mr. Ratnesh Mishra
idym.designation: Co-Founder & Principal Investigator, IDYM Foundation (ISRO's Space Tutor)

partners.name: DS Brar Center for Girls and Women in STEM

studentCorner.heading: Continue Your Science Journey
studentCorner.text: Did the van visit your school? Want to keep learning robotics, space science and more? Fill the form and we will add you to a student group for continuous training and possible visits to our hubs.

contact.address: Plaksha University, Alpha Sector 101, IT City Road, S.A.S. Nagar, Punjab 140306
